title: simarouba glauca subsp. latifolia
intro: Simarouba glauca subsp. latifolia, also known as the broadleaf simarouba, is a subspecies of the Simarouba glauca tree, native to Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, derived from its leaves, bark, and roots. The health benefits of Simarouba glauca subsp. latifolia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, as well as its use as an anti-parasitic agent. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
